items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+500 to +550):
items.body: The clear betting favorite entering the Masters, with elite tee-to-green form and the kind of consistency that makes him a constant threat at Augusta National.

items.label: Rory McIlroy (+1100 to +1200):
items.body: The defending Masters champion after completing the career Grand Slam last year, and still one of the most dangerous players in the field when his driver and irons are both working.

items.label: Bryson DeChambeau (+1000):
items.body: A power-heavy contender who can take advantage of Augusta’s scoring holes, especially if he keeps the long game in control.

items.label: Jon Rahm (+1000 to +1100):
items.body: A proven major champion with the all-around game to contend at Augusta, where precision, patience, and creativity are all at a premium.

items.label: Xander Schauffele (+1400 to +1600):
items.body: Consistently one of the most reliable major championship performers, with a balanced game that fits the demands of Augusta National.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+1600):
items.body: One of the most talented young ball strikers in the field, capable of separating from the pack if the short game holds up.

items.label: Tommy Fleetwood (+2200):
items.body: An accurate, polished all-around player whose tee-to-green consistency gives him real upside on a course that punishes mistakes.

items.label: Matt Fitzpatrick (+2200):
items.body: A strong fit for Augusta’s demanding approach shots and subtle greens, with enough course-management skill to stay in contention.

items.label: Hideki Matsuyama (+2500):
items.body: A past Masters champion whose elite iron play and comfort on the course make him a dangerous mid-range option.

items.label: Defending Champion Returns:
items.body: Rory McIlroy arrives at Augusta as the reigning Masters champion, bringing extra attention after completing the career Grand Slam last year.

items.label: Scheffler Leads Market:
items.body: Scottie Scheffler is the clear betting favorite this week, reflecting how dominant his tee-to-green profile has been entering the tournament.

items.label: Big Names Missing:
items.body: Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson are out, removing two of the most recognizable names from the field.

items.label: Subtle Course Change:
items.body: Augusta National lengthened the 17th hole by 10 yards for 2026, adding a small but notable wrinkle to an already demanding closing stretch.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Rory McIlroy won last year’s Masters and returns to defend the title.

items.label: Recent Masters Narrative:
items.body: The Masters continues to reward a complete game, with elite ball striking, distance control, and composure under pressure usually separating the winner from the rest.

items.label: Field Strength:
items.body: The 2026 field includes multiple major winners and a deep top tier, keeping the event among the strongest in golf.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: Augusta National Golf Club, Augusta, Georgia.

items.label: Course Setup:
items.body: The course plays at 7,565 yards in 2026 after the 17th hole was lengthened by 10 yards.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: The tournament runs Thursday, April 9 through Sunday, April 12, 2026.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 72-hole stroke play with the traditional 36-hole cut.

items.label: TV Coverage:
items.body: ESPN carries Thursday and Friday coverage, while CBS handles the weekend broadcast windows.

items.label: Streaming:
items.body: Masters.com, the Masters app, and Paramount+ are among the main streaming options.

items.label: Par 3 Contest:
items.body: The Par 3 Contest is scheduled for Wednesday, April 8, as part of Masters week.
